results.possibleIdentity: Richard B. Ahlers was a mechanical engineering student at New York University (NYU) during the late 1960s and early 1970s. He was based at NYU's University Heights campus in the Bronx (ZIP code 10453), which housed the university's College of Engineering until 1973. Ahlers was associated with Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.), a non-profit organization established in 1966 to foster collaborations between artists and engineers. His professional and contact information was maintained in the E.A.T. technical person index, which served as a registry for engineers willing to provide technical assistance to artists. Later in his career, he appears to have been based in Texas, where a Richard B. Ahlers is listed in engineering-related federal documents and whose estate was active in the Houston area as of 2021.
